questions: You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. You're sitting in a waiting room at the dentist with nothing to do.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. It's a Saturday afternoon and you have no plans.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. You're eating breakfast alone before work.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. You're riding the bus on your commute home.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. You've finished dinner and are sitting on the couch with nothing particular in mind.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. You're waiting for a friend who's running fifteen minutes late at a café.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. You're lying in bed at night but not quite ready to sleep.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. You have a free hour between meetings and nothing urgent to do.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. You're sitting on a park bench during your lunch break.
You find it hard to sit still with your thoughts — you always feel a little restless. It starts raining while you're home on a Sunday and your errands are done.
